64.reason: [The post-increment operator high++ passes the current value of high to the recursive call, then increments high. This means the right child call uses the incremented value, but the left child call uses the original value, causing incorrect height propagation. Should use high+1 instead to pass the correct height to both children.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Post-increment passes the current value (high) and then increments the local variable, causing left child to receive unchanged depth and right child to receive an incremented value that is still one less than correct. Should be high+1 instead.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect depth propagation: post-increment passes current high to left child, then increments local high, causing left child to receive one less than the correct depth. Should be high+1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Using post-increment (high++) passes the current value of 'high' to the recursive call and increments it afterwards. This means both left and right children receive the same height value, and the height is not correctly propagated down the tree. It should be 'high + 1'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Using post-increment (high++) passes the current value of high to the recursive call, then increments the local variable. This means both left and right children receive the same height value. It should be high+1 to pass the incremented height without modifying the local variable or affecting the sibling call.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using post-increment (high++) passes the current value of 'high' to the left subtree and then increments it locally, but the right subtree call uses the already incremented value incorrectly. reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: post-increment modifies high after passing it, leading to wrong height values in deeper recursive calls. Should use high+1.]
